decks.notes: This deck focuses on aggressive gameplay by utilizing a variety of aggressive creatures and spells to put pressure on the opponent. It also incorporates Leyline cards to maximize the effectiveness of the creatures and spells played. The deck includes powerful cards such as Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Scion of Draco to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The deck also includes control elements like Stubborn Denial and Teferi, Time Raveler to protect its creatures and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient threats.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. The deck aims to efficiently remove threats using cards like Fatal Push and Sheoldred's Edict, while also countering key spells with cards like Counterspell and Spell Snare. Additionally, the deck utilizes card draw with spells like Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian to maintain card advantage and outvalue the opponent. The goal is to outlast the opponent and win through attrition and resource management.

The deck focuses on utilizing the synergy between cards like Amulet of Vigor, Primeval Titan, and Lotus Field to quickly ramp up mana and fetch key lands like Urza's Saga and Boseiju, Who Endures. It aims to generate a large amount of mana to cast spells like Scapeshift and Malevolent Rumble, enabling the player to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent with big creatures and powerful abilities. The deck is built around the concept of fast mana generation and explosive combo plays to dominate the game.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with creatures and then use Living End to bring them all back onto the battlefield. The deck includes cards like Shardless Agent and Force of Negation to help control the game until the combo can be assembled. Other cards like Misty Rainforest and Polluted Delta help to thin out the deck and find the necessary pieces. With cards like Overlord of the Balemurk and Endurance to support the combo, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a powerful board presence from the graveyard.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board and resources through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria. It also includes versatile spells like Galvanic Discharge and No More Lies to deal with threats efficiently. Additionally, it has access to powerful finishers like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Wrath of the Skies to close out games. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing its own dominance on the battlefield, ultimately leading to victory.

This deck focuses on playing aggressive creatures such as Scion of Draco, Territorial Kavu, Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, and Doorkeeper Thrull, supported by Leyline Binding and Leyline of the Guildpact to control the board. The key strategy is to maximize damage output through the use of Tribal Flames and Lightning Bolt while also utilizing Stubborn Denial for protection. Arena of Glory provides additional support for the aggressive creatures, creating a fast-paced and efficient aggro gameplay to overwhelm the opponent in a Modern 1 vs 1 format.

This deck revolves around quickly building a board presence and dealing damage to the opponent with a combination of creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to play aggressively with cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride, while also using Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge to further pressure the opponent. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury provide additional threats, while cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Thraben Charm offer utility and card advantage. The mana base is optimized for consistency and includes disruption elements like Blood Moon to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on using the Cascade mechanic to cast Living End from the graveyard, bringing back powerful creatures while disrupting the opponent's game plan with Force of Negation and Subtlety. Shardless Agent and Generous Ent help cycle through the deck efficiently, while Endurance and Otawara, Soaring City provide graveyard hate. Curator of Mysteries and Overlord of the Balemurk offer additional win conditions, with support from Formidable Speaker and Deceit. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful board presence.

This deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Urza's Power Plant, and Urza's Mine) to generate enormous amounts of mana to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures and planeswalker spells. The goal is to control the game by deploying threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Thought-Knot Seer, while disrupting opponents with cards like Kozilek's Command and Karn, the Great Creator. The deck also includes cards like Ugin, the Eye of the Storms and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger for additional late-game power. Expedition Map is used to tutor for specific lands, ensuring a consistent mana base.

This deck focuses on utilizing a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells to control the game and put pressure on the opponent. By deploying powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd alongside removal spells like Prismatic Ending and Galvanic Discharge, the deck seeks to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own strategy for victory. The inclusion of cards like Teferi, Time Raveler also helps to provide additional disruption and control elements to the deck, making it a versatile and challenging opponent to face.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays and burn spells to quickly damage the opponent and control the board. The key strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, while using burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to clear the way and finish off opponents. The inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ocelot Pride provide additional threat and advantage for aggressive plays. The deck also includes some disruptive elements like Static Prison and Blood Moon to slow down opponents and secure victory.

This deck focuses on maximizing the benefits of having multiple colored permanents on the battlefield, with Leyline of the Guildpact acting as a key card to boost the deck's power. The deck aims to quickly establish a board presence with aggressive creatures like Scion of Draco, Territorial Kavu, and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, while also utilizing control elements like Stubborn Denial and Lightning Bolt to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of cards like Leyline Binding and Doorkeeper Thrull help to further support the aggro strategy, making the most of the deck's diverse mana base.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Amulet of Vigor and Primeval Titan to quickly ramp up mana and pull out powerful lands such as Urza's Saga and Lotus Field. By combining land tutoring cards like Summoner's Pact and Tolaria West with land bounce and untap effects, the deck can quickly assemble a strong board state and overwhelm opponents with powerful land interactions, allowing for explosive plays and a fast win condition. The key strategy is to efficiently utilize land searching and bouncing effects to chain together plays and overwhelm the opponent with big threats.

The main concept of this deck is to generate mana quickly and efficiently using cards like Manamorphose, Pyretic Ritual, and Ruby Medallion to cast powerful spells like Reckless Impulse and Ral, Monsoon Mage. The deck looks to combo off by chaining spells together with the help of Past in Flames to ultimately finish the game with a storm win condition using cards like Grapeshot. The deck aims to control the tempo of the game while setting up for explosive turns to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

The key strategy of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana with Utopia Sprawl and Greenbelt Rampager to cast powerful combo pieces like Dour Port-Mage and Laboratory Maniac. Utilize Shardless Agent and Primal Prayers to dig for key cards while staying versatile with counter spells like Force of Negation and Flare of Denial. Use snow lands and Ice-Fang Coatl for additional control and card draw. With the right setup, the deck can quickly assemble its combo pieces and outmaneuver opponents to achieve victory.

The main concept of this deck is to control the board and ramp into powerful Eldrazi creatures. By using cards like Sowing Mycospawn and Utopia Sprawl to ramp up mana quickly, you can play big threats like Sire of Seven Deaths and Devourer of Destiny. Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble help control the opponent's board while Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Emrakul, the Promised End provide late-game power. The deck also includes utility lands like Sanctum of Ugin and Ghost Quarter to further control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy.
